ddls:
CREATE TABLE "incidents" (
  "incident_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each incident */,
  "incident_type" INTEGER /* Type of incident (e.g., accident, near-miss) */,
  "severity" INTEGER /* Severity of the incident (e.g., fatal, non-fatal) */,
  "location" INTEGER /* Location of the incident (e.g., urban, rural) */,
  "trip_duration" REAL /* Duration of the trip related to the incident */,
  "avg_ut" REAL /* Average speed during the trip */,
  "incident_date" TEXT /* Date of the incident */,
  "incident_time" TEXT /* Time of the incident */,
  "weather_conditions" TEXT /* Weather conditions at the time of the incident */,
  "traffic_conditions" TEXT /* Traffic conditions at the time of the incident */,
  "road_conditions" TEXT /* Road conditions at the time of the incident */,
  "vehicle_id" INTEGER /* ID of the vehicle involved in the incident */,
  "driver_id" INTEGER /* ID of the driver involved in the incident */,
  "incident_description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the incident */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("incident_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_incidents_severity FOREIGN KEY ("severity") REFERENCES severity_levels ("severity_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_incidents_driver_id FOREIGN KEY ("driver_id") REFERENCES drivers ("driver_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_incidents_vehicle_id FOREIGN KEY ("vehicle_id") REFERENCES vehicles ("vehicle_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_incidents_location FOREIGN KEY ("location") REFERENCES locations ("location_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_incidents_incident_type FOREIGN KEY ("incident_type") REFERENCES incident_types ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "incident_types" (
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each incident type */,
  "type_name" TEXT /* Name of the incident type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the incident type */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of the incident type (e.g., traffic, pedestrian) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "locations" (
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each location */,
  "location_name" TEXT /* Name of the location */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the location */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude of the location */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude of the location */,
  "street_name" TEXT /* Street name where the incident occurred */,
  "city" TEXT /* City where the incident occurred */,
  "state" TEXT /* State where the incident occurred */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("location_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "severity_levels" (
  "severity_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each severity level */,
  "severity_name" TEXT /* Name of the severity level */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the severity level */,
  "impact_level" TEXT /* Impact level of the severity (e.g., major, minor) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("severity_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "trip_data" (
  "trip_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each trip */,
  "incident_id" INTEGER /* ID of the incident related to the trip */,
  "trip_duration" REAL /* Duration of the trip */,
  "avg_ut" REAL /* Average speed during the trip */,
  "start_point" TEXT /* Start point of the trip */,
  "end_point" TEXT /* End point of the trip */,
  "route" TEXT /* Route taken during the trip */,
  "distance" REAL /* Distance traveled during the trip */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("trip_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_trip_data_incident_id FOREIGN KEY ("incident_id") REFERENCES incidents ("incident_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "fatalities" (
  "fatality_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each fatality */,
  "incident_id" INTEGER /* ID of the incident related to the fatality */,
  "n_fat_in" INTEGER /* Number of fatalities inside the vehicle */,
  "n_fat_out" INTEGER /* Number of fatalities outside the vehicle */,
  "age" INTEGER /* Age of the fatality */,
  "gender" TEXT /* Gender of the fatality */,
  "cause_of_death" TEXT /* Cause of death */,
  "vehicle_type" TEXT /* Type of vehicle involved */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("fatality_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_fatalities_incident_id FOREIGN KEY ("incident_id") REFERENCES incidents ("incident_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "injuries" (
  "injury_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each injury */,
  "incident_id" INTEGER /* ID of the incident related to the injury */,
  "n_inj_in" INTEGER /* Number of injuries inside the vehicle */,
  "n_inj_out" INTEGER /* Number of injuries outside the vehicle */,
  "age" INTEGER /* Age of the injured person */,
  "gender" TEXT /* Gender of the injured person */,
  "injury_type" TEXT /* Type of injury */,
  "vehicle_type" TEXT /* Type of vehicle involved */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("injury_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_injuries_incident_id FOREIGN KEY ("incident_id") REFERENCES incidents ("incident_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., analyst, planner, administrator) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department of the user */,
  "contact_number" TEXT /* Contact number of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "incident_id" INTEGER /* ID of the incident being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the incident */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the incident was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user */,
  "device" TEXT /* Device used to access the data */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_incident_id FOREIGN KEY ("incident_id") REFERENCES incidents ("incident_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "vehicles" (
  "vehicle_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each vehicle */,
  "vehicle_type" TEXT /* Type of vehicle (e.g., car, truck, bike) */,
  "make" TEXT /* Make of the vehicle */,
  "model" TEXT /* Model of the vehicle */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year of the vehicle */,
  "license_plate" TEXT /* License plate number of the vehicle */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("vehicle_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "drivers" (
  "driver_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each driver */,
  "driver_name" TEXT /* Name of the driver */,
  "license_number" TEXT /* License number of the driver */,
  "age" INTEGER /* Age of the driver */,
  "gender" TEXT /* Gender of the driver */,
  "contact_number" TEXT /* Contact number of the driver */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("driver_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "weather_reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each weather report */,
  "location_id" INTEGER /* ID of the location */,
  "report_date" TEXT /* Date of the weather report */,
  "report_time" TEXT /* Time of the weather report */,
  "temperature" REAL /* Temperature at the time of the report */,
  "precipitation" REAL /* Precipitation at the time of the report */,
  "wind_speed" REAL /* Wind speed at the time of the report */,
  "humidity" REAL /* Humidity at the time of the report */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_weather_reports_location_id FOREIGN KEY ("location_id") REFERENCES locations ("location_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "traffic_reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each traffic report */,
  "location_id" INTEGER /* ID of the location */,
  "report_date" TEXT /* Date of the traffic report */,
  "report_time" TEXT /* Time of the traffic report */,
  "traffic_volume" INTEGER /* Traffic volume at the time of the report */,
  "average_speed" REAL /* Average speed of traffic at the time of the report */,
  "congestion_level" TEXT /* Congestion level at the time of the report (e.g., light, heavy) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_traffic_reports_location_id FOREIGN KEY ("location_id") REFERENCES locations ("location_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "road_conditions" (
  "condition_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each road condition report */,
  "location_id" INTEGER /* ID of the location */,
  "report_date" TEXT /* Date of the road condition report */,
  "report_time" TEXT /* Time of the road condition report */,
  "surface_condition" TEXT /* Surface condition of the road (e.g., dry, wet) */,
  "lighting_condition" TEXT /* Lighting condition of the road (e.g., well-lit, poorly lit) */,
  "maintenance_status" TEXT /* Maintenance status of the road (e.g., good, needs repair) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("condition_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_road_conditions_location_id FOREIGN KEY ("location_id") REFERENCES locations ("location_id")
)
